verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family tyrosine kinase, N-terminally myristoylated and palmitoylated, canonically anchored to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane and intracellular signaling. Baseline localization is cytoplasmic-face / inner-leaflet. However, ecto-LYN has been documented on the surface of certain cancer cells (notably AML and some solid tumor contexts) as part of the broader ecto-kinase / ecto-Src-family phenomenon in oncogenic states, qualifying as cell-state-induced surface display. Not GPI/TM anchored; not a stable complex partner of a surface protein at baseline; not lysosomal; no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering; not narrowly germline-restricted.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Strength of ecto-LYN evidence on tumor cell surfaces is limited; could alternatively be called `no`/`inner_leaflet_anchored` if cancer ecto-pool is discounted.
